Off Calendar Jan-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT S. NELSON, Esq. of Nelson Law Group (Tel: 415-702-9869) for plaintiff Ravneet Kaur, M.D.;
Atty. NEDA HEFZI, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Kathleen M. Welsh, M.D., P.C.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT KATHLEEN M. WELSH, M.D., P.C.'s Motion For Protective Order And For Return Of Inadvertently Disclosed Attorney-Client Communications.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Motion granted. It is undisputed that the Abruzzo email is a privileged communication. See Hefzi Decl., Ex E. Defendant has established that its disclosure was unintentional, and defense counsel promptly notified plaintiff's counsel once she learned it had been inadvertently produced with the personnel file. Plaintiff's counsel did not follow the protocol in State Compensation Ins. Fund v. WPS, Inc. (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 644, but instead retained the information for over a year and sought to leverage it in the litigation. Plaintiff's counsel shall promptly return the Abruzzo email and destroy any copies. Plaintiff's counsel Robert S. Nelson shall pay sanctions in the amount of $2,500 to defendant's counsel Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP, within 30 days.

Counsel for defendant to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.
